description_long: This object is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a straight, rectangular wing mounted at the midsection of the fuselage. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers toward the nose, which is rounded and likely houses the cockpit. The airplane features a single propeller mounted at the rear of the fuselage in a pusher configuration. The tail assembly is distinctive, with twin booms extending from the wings to support a horizontal stabilizer and a single vertical fin, forming an H-tail design. The surface appears to be smooth and metallic, with subtle panel lines and dark accent markings on the wingtips.
description: A small, streamlined airplane with a mid-mounted straight wing, rear pusher propeller, and H-tail supported by twin booms.
description_view_0: Front view showing the straight wing, central fuselage, and twin booms extending rearward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the streamlined nose, mid-mounted wing, and the rear propeller behind the fuselage.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the pusher propeller, H-tail configuration, and the twin booms supporting the tail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the same features as view 1, with clear visibility of the tail structure and wing.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing, straight rectangular wings with no visible dihedral or winglets.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin booms and a single vertical stabilizer.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Not visibly extended; landing gear type not clearly shown.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit integrated into the nose of the fuselage.
